title: Ola vs. Sven
synopsis: Ola vs. Sven, Norway vs. Sweden — who is the best ski nation?
synopsis_no: Ola mot Sven, Norge mot Sverige — hvem er den beste skinasjonen?
visual_style: Minimal, iconic clear-line European comic style in the tradition of Franco-Belgian bande dessinee. Uniform line weight throughout — clean, confident ink outlines with no sketching, no variable brush strokes, no hatching, no crosshatching. Flat colors only — no gradients, no painterly shading, no complex lighting. Characters have minimal facial features: simple dot or oval eyes, a small nose, and a small mouth — maximum 3–6 facial lines excluding the head outline. No wrinkles, skin texture, pores, stubble, or realistic shading on faces or bodies. Hair is rendered as one clean graphic shape with at most 1–2 internal lines. Bodies have clean, readable silhouettes with slightly simplified, stylized human proportions — not exaggerated caricature, not anatomically detailed. No detailed musculature or anatomy rendering. Racing suits are drawn with very limited clothing folds — only essential lines to suggest the onepiece synthetic material, no realistic fabric texture. Hands are expressive but simplified. The setting is a bright winter landscape: white snow, blue sky, sunshine. Backgrounds are flat, simple, and secondary to characters — snow-covered terrain, sparse pine trees, gentle hills rendered as clean color shapes, never competing with the two skiers for attention. The two characters dominate every panel. Camera angles vary for visual energy: close-ups of faces, full-body action shots, and occasional bird's-eye views of the course. Color palette is dominated by white snow, bright blue sky, and the bold costume colors of the two skiers. Strictly avoid: photorealism, semi-realistic comic rendering, 3D rendering, glossy gradients, painterly or digital illustration style, manga or anime style, realistic fabric texture, heavy shadow modeling, detailed teeth or gums or facial structure, complex lighting, textured brushes, variable-weight ink lines, overly detailed backgrounds, dark or moody lighting, any resemblance to existing copyrighted comic characters, and any style that pulls focus away from the characters.

locations.name: The Cross-Country Course
locations.description: A snowy cross-country skiing course on a bright, sunny winter day. The terrain varies between open snow fields, gentle hills, forest sections with tall pine trees, and narrow trail passages — but the focus is always on the two skiers, not the scenery.

characters.name: The Norwegian
characters.description: A Norwegian cross-country skier in his late 20s. Short brown hair rendered as one clean graphic shape. Minimal facial features: simple eyes, small nose, small mouth — expressive but simplified. He wears a red onepiece synthetic ski racing suit drawn with very few fold lines. He carries ski poles and wears racing skis. Clean, readable silhouette. Solid build, good posture, energetic but not action-hero-like. No detailed musculature, no realistic shading.

characters.name: The Swede
characters.description: A Swedish cross-country skier in his late 20s. Blonde hair poking out from under his ski headband, rendered as one clean graphic shape. Minimal facial features: simple eyes, small nose, small mouth — expressive but simplified. He wears a blue and yellow onepiece synthetic ski racing suit drawn with very few fold lines. He carries ski poles and wears racing skis. Clean, readable silhouette. Tall and lean, smooth skiing form, annoyingly relaxed. No detailed musculature, no realistic shading.

rules: The race never ends. The Norwegian and The Swede are always mid-competition, always neck-and-neck. No one ever crosses a finish line. The race simply continues forever.

Sabotage never fully works. When The Norwegian or The Swede attempts to sabotage the other — no matter how extreme (bazookas, catapults, avalanches, moose stampedes) — the victim always survives and recovers cartoonishly. The sabotage may slow them down, knock them sideways, or create chaos, but they always get back on their skis and the race continues.

Sabotage is roughly 50/50. Sometimes The Norwegian attacks and The Swede must deal with it. Other times, The Swede strikes first with a cunning trick, and the user must figure out how The Norwegian responds. The LLM should regularly have The Swede initiate sabotage to keep the user on their toes.

The humor is rooted in Norway vs. Sweden rivalry — friendly, never mean-spirited. Trash talk draws on real cultural stereotypes: Norwegian brown cheese and oil money vs. Swedish meatballs and IKEA furniture. Both characters are proud of their country and love to mock the other's.

The tone is absurd, escalating, and cartoonish — think Wile E. Coyote on skis. Sabotage methods can be wildly unrealistic (rocket-powered skis, trained eagles, banana peels on snow) but the characters always react with determined sportsmanship and get back to racing.

Dialogue between The Norwegian and The Swede should be punchy trash talk, competitive banter, and nationalistic one-liners. They mock each other constantly but there's an underlying mutual respect buried deep beneath the rivalry.

Narration is delivered in the voice of an increasingly bewildered sports commentator — professional broadcasting tone struggling to maintain composure while describing someone firing a bazooka on a ski trail. Think: 'And Nordmann appears to be... unpacking what seems to be a medieval trebuchet. This is... unprecedented in Olympic history.'

The narrator should never reference the race as 'eternal' or 'never-ending' or hint that the race cannot be won. The endless race is the comic's structural premise, not something characters or the narrator are aware of. The narrator simply commentates on what's happening right now as if it's a normal (albeit chaotic) competition.
long_term_narrative: The sabotage methods escalate in absurdity over time — from tripping with ski poles to increasingly ridiculous and creative schemes.
Despite the chaos, both skiers remain neck-and-neck. Every advantage is temporary. The eternal race continues.
narrative_premise: The Norwegian and The Swede are locked in the most intense cross-country ski race in history — and it will never end. They are neck-and-neck, and both will resort to increasingly absurd sabotage to gain the upper hand. But nothing ever works for long. Bazookas misfire, avalanches are dodged, rocket skis explode at the wrong moment. The race always continues, the rivalry never dies, and the trash talk between Norway and Sweden flows as freely as the snow.
